title: One-lap hazard reformulation with survival-consistent features
summary: Pit-on-next-lap is a discrete-time event decision, so a hazard-style model should fit the label structure better than a generic tabular classifier.
rationale: Pit-on-next-lap is a discrete-time event decision, so a hazard-style model should fit the label structure better than a generic tabular classifier. Discrete-time survival literature emphasizes modeling event probability conditional on survival so far, which matches lap-by-lap pit timing.
implementation_hint: Create a specialist model trained only on pre-pit laps, with features like stint age, laps remaining, degradation slope proxies, and hazard-baseline bins for lap-in-stint and race progress. Keep CV grouped by race-year and ordered within driver-race so any baseline hazard table is estimated only from earlier rows/folds.
expected_effect: Better ranking around borderline stop-now vs wait-one-lap cases; likely small CV lift if current model underuses sequential event structure.
risk: Easy to leak if hazard priors or per-bin rates are computed on full races or full folds; also may add little if the wrapper already captures most nonlinearities.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
